title: Karma Yoga

principle.text:
The question Shreyas is really asking is not about productivity. It is about identity. High agency is not a trait you either have or do not have. It is a habit of defaulting to action over permission-seeking. Most people wait for the right role, the right signal, the right moment to call themselves a PM. The waiting feels like prudence. It is usually avoidance.

For someone in the in-between, CSM by title and PM by aspiration, high agency shows up in the small things. The spec drafted without being asked. The one-pager written on a Friday because you were curious. The customer problem framed as a hypothesis instead of a complaint. Nobody will hand you permission to become what you are trying to become. The slot does not open because you waited well.

The question for today is not whether you are ready. It is what you would do if you assumed you were.

move: Take a real customer problem from this week, one ticket, one pattern, one friction you heard. Write a one-page product brief: the problem in one sentence, one piece of evidence, who has it, why it matters, and the roughest possible solution shape. Keep it private. The goal is not to impress anyone. It is to prove to yourself you can do the thinking.
question: What is one thing you have been waiting for permission to start?
